old_claim.note: cas9_programmable_nuclease was previously treated as a plausible hard prerequisite for pam_recognition_constraint without a field-specific edge receipt.

new_claim.note: PAM recognition is a Cas9 target-DNA recognition constraint rather than a separate engineered variant.

source_shape.source_locator: Structural analysis of Cas9 activation and DNA recognition, including PAM-dependent target engagement by Cas9 endonucleases.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source places PAM recognition inside Cas9 target recognition, showing the constraint is a property of the programmable Cas9 nuclease system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The edge is required because this node is scoped to the Cas9 PAM constraint itself, not to an independent motif-search technology or downstream application.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: pam_recognition_constraint -> cas9_programmable_nuclease remains required; this receipt adds source-shaped evidence for the existing CRISPR field edge.
would_reject_if: The source did not connect PAM recognition to Cas9 nuclease targeting.
The node scope broadened to generic DNA motif recognition while keeping this required edge.
The edge was treated as a historical predecessor rather than an architectural dependency.
short_reason: The PAM-recognition constraint is modeled as a Cas9 target-recognition property.
